title: #2 - What legal documents do I need to provide?
bodyHtml:
What legal documents do I need to provide?

Uploading your legal documents is one of the most important parts of the onboarding process since you will have to provide proof of your commercial activities.

 

Let’s go step by step: 

 

Step 1: Fill in your legal details.

In this part you’ll need to add the legal name of your company, and you’ll need to make sure that the name of your company matches the name in your company registration document.

 

You’ll also need to add your company registration number which may be completely different from your VAT number. 

You’ll find this number in your company registration document.

 

Here’s an example:

 

Step 2: Fill in your tax details.

You’ll need to indicate whether your company is located in the EU or not.

 

If your company is located in the EU:

 

  • Indicate which country you’re shipping from.

  • Add your company VAT number or indicate if you’re exempt from the VAT number

  • Specify which VAT scheme applies to the products you want to sell.

 

If your company is located outside of the EU:

 

  • Indicate which country you’re shipping from.

  • Add your VAT, TIN, EIN, or Tax ID number. If you’re exempt, you may leave the field empty, but in this case we’ll follow up to ask why. Reminder: If you are VAT exempt, please upload the UTR document or any other document proving that you are eligible for VAT exemption.

  • Indicate if you have a permanent establishment in the EU.

 

Now that you’ve filled in all of your info, select “Add tax information”. If you select “save” the task will show as “To do” instead of “Done”.

 

Step 3: Add your EPR documents.

What are these documents? Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is an environmental policy that makes the party who first places a product on the market subject to EPR requirements. This party is also known as the “producer”.

 

 

Germany

If you want to sell in Germany, an EPR registration is mandatory.

 

Here are the 3 numbers you’ll need:

 

  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)
  • Packaging
  • Batteries


To get your numbers for Germany, go to these third parties:

 

  • For Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), get your number here: https://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home
  • For Packaging, get your number here: https://lucid.verpackungsregister.org/
  • For Batteries, get your number here: https://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home

If you are exempt from WEEE or Batteries, please tell Back Market by ticking the exemption box. Please note you cannot be exempt from Packaging.

 

France

If you sell in France, you will need EPR registrations for:

 

  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)
  • Packaging
  • Batteries

If you are exempt from WEEE or Batteries, please tell Back Market by ticking the exemption box. Please note you cannot be exempt from Packaging. 

 

But if you do not input your EPR registration numbers or, if applicable, your exemption status, Back Market will report EPR fees on your behalf and charge you for the associated fees in your invoices.

 

Spain

If you are a Spanish business, you must comply with Spain Packaging EPR regulations ; BM cannot collect on behalf of Spanish sellers. You may provide your EPR number in your Back Office but it’s not mandatory. 

 

If you are not a Spanish business, you must provide your EPR packaging registration number if you hold one. If you do not hold this registration, Back Market will report EPR packaging on your behalf and will charge the corresponding amounts to your account. 

 

UK 

Currently Back Market only authorities UK sellers to sell on the UK platform. UK sellers must comply with applicable EPR regulations and notably WEEE. If you are a UK seller, you may provide us with your EPR registration numbers in your Back Office but it’s not mandatory.

 

Please note Back Market is not allowed to collect and report UK EPR fees on your behalf.

 

Austria

Sellers active on the Austria platform must comply with applicable EPR regulations and notably WEEE. You may provide us with your EPR registration numbers in your Back Office but it’s not mandatory. Please note Back Market is not allowed to collect and report Austria EPR fees on your behalf. 

 

Learn more about EPR documents in France here  

 

Learn more about EPR documents in Germany here

 

If you need more information about EPR numbers, contact the Activation Team. 

 

Step 4: For EU sellers only: Add your country of tax residence. If you’re a seller from the UK, leave this section blank.

 

 

Choose your country from the countries displayed and add other permanent establishments (if you have any). Then add your Tax Identification Number (TIN). Remember that this is for EU sellers only.

 

 

Step 5: Upload your legal documents.

This part is the most important of the legal section. All your documents will be confirmed by our own internal team.

 

This is how the section looks before uploading the documents.

 

 

Here’s what we ask for:

 

  • Banking information 

Make sure that you upload a proper bank document. We won’t accept a screenshot from your smartphone, it has to be a document that clearly shows both the BIC and the IBAN numbers, and that are under the name of the company registering.

You’ll need to add the same IBAN and BIC in your payment methods so that the information in your Back Office and Hyperwallet (transfer methods) matches (we’ll see this in the payment section).

 

  • Memorandum and Articles of Association

The Memorandum of Association document sets out the company's fundamental characteristics and objectives. It includes details such as the company's name, registered office address, objectives or purposes for which the company is formed, and details of the initial shareholders or members. The memorandum defines the scope of the company's activities and serves as a constitutional document outlining the company's legal capacity and powers.

 

The Articles of Association are the internal rules and regulations governing the management and operation of the company. They detail how the company will be run, including procedures for shareholder meetings, appointment and powers of directors, issuance and transfer of shares, and distribution of profits. Articles of Association complement the Memorandum of Association by providing specific guidelines for the company's internal operations and administration.

These documents are usually created at the same time the company is created. In France, it’s known as “Status de la societé”.

 

  • Legal owner’s government-issued ID

You’ll need to upload a picture of the official ID (front and back) of one of the owners of the company. Make sure that the ID isn’t expired. An official ID can be a passport, driver’s license, national identity card, visa, or residence permit. Back Market will ask the ID owner to perform an ID check later in the process.

 

  • Certificate of Incorporation

This is a legal document that establishes the existence of a corporation or company. It includes important information about the company, such as its legal name, address, business purpose, ownership structure, and often sets out the rights and responsibilities of shareholders and directors.

 

This certificate is issued by the government agency responsible for registering companies in the jurisdiction where the company is incorporated.

 

You’ll need to make sure that your certificate of incorporation is not less than 3 months old.

 

 

REMINDER: You need to upload the complete document and not only the first page 

 

Here are examples of how your certificate of incorporation should look like depending on the country your company is based:

 

In France, this document is called the KBIS. (Note that freelancers or companies with no legal entity can provide an INSEE.)

 

 

In Spain, this document is called the Extracto de Registro Mercantil.

 

 

In Germany, this document is called the Auszug aus dem Handelsregister.

 

 

In the Netherlands, this document is called the KVK - Kamer van Koophandel.

 

In Belgium, this document is called the KBO/BCE.

 

 

In Italy, this document is called the Visura Camerale - “Visura Ordinaria Societa’ Di Capitale”.

 

In the UK, this document is called the certified copy of the company certificate from the Companies House. We can also ask for the confirmation statement CS01 for a more recent proof of business.

 

 

To learn more about how to set up your Back Office account, go to the next article.

 

REMINDER: 

Your listings and your account will appear as “Offline” until your account will be validated by the Activation team.

 

Once you have completed all the sections of the onboarding, please send an email to activation-eu@backmarket.com

 

Need help? Don't hesitate to reach out to the Back Market team at activation-eu@backmarket.com
